reason: The original E280 continuous-RX sweeps failed after their first power point because the modem AT firmware rewrote unchanged persistent settings and the radio refused the next configuration transition. The nine 60-second RX recovery points completed in independent processes and were merged by rate. Six packet points also contained shortened transmissions: subsequent diagnostics found that a failed persistent setter could leave the ESP32 shadow configuration ahead of the physical E280, and that this pair periodically truncated a frame without an inter-run reset. The firmware now rolls back the shadow configuration after a failed verified write, both radios are read back before recovery, and AT+RESET is executed and the full configuration restored between measured packets. The six packet overrides below each contain five delivered, event-stable repetitions.
